steps.title: Spot the causal setup
steps.content: The passage explains that Bell and Carver adapted a specific practice; the follow-up sentence reveals what this adaptation enabled. A transition showing cause-and-effect is required.

steps.title: Apply the cause-effect transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect: Bell and Carver adopted a shared pseudonym, and as a result they were able to express their joint creative vision. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence is a consequence of the first.
  • Choice A — ‘In this way,’: signals that what follows is the result or outcome of the preceding method, fitting perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘For instance,’: signals an illustrative example, so fails — the second sentence is a consequence, not a separate example of the same point. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Later,’: signals a later point in time, so fails — no time gap is implied between adopting the pseudonym and what it enabled. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘On the other hand,’: signals contrast, so fails — the second sentence continues the same idea rather than opposing it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
